title: Crepidomanes longilabiatum
intro: Crepidomanes longilabiatum, also known as the Long-labiate Creeping Fern, is a type of terrestrial fern that belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidomanes longilabiatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Crepidomanes longilabiatum is often made into herbal tea, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Crepidomanes longilabiatum is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the filmy ferns. This family is part of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns. The Hymenophyllaceae family is further divided into several genera, with Crepidomanes being one of them. The classification of Crepidomanes longilabiatum is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Hymenophyllales, Family: Hymenophyllaceae, Genus: Crepidomanes, and Species: C. longilabiatum. This classification highlights the fern's place within the plant kingdom and its unique characteristics.
